entities.name: Dulce Base
entities.context: Schneider claimed to have helped build alleged 7-level joint human/non-human facility; died under suspicious circumstances Dec 1996.
entities.notes: Dulce Base Wikipedia article body has no photographs. Candidates from Category:Dulce,_New_Mexico — area views of the town/reservation, distinct from the round-1 'Welcome to Dulce' sign.

entities.name: Area 51 / Groom Lake
entities.context: Existence officially acknowledged 2013. CIA declassified history confirms U-2/OXCART/F-117 testing.

entities.name: Los Alamos National Laboratory
entities.context: Manhattan Project origin. Extensive underground testing and storage facilities. Active nuclear weapons research.

entities.name: Sandia National Laboratories
entities.context: Nuclear weapons engineering center. Manzano Base adjacent — confirmed underground nuclear weapons storage since 1950s.
entities.notes: Sandia Labs Wikipedia article body is image-poor. Solar tower/heliostat field is the most recognizable Sandia facility imagery. Aerials show the Kirtland co-location and Sandia ridge.

entities.name: Denver International Airport
entities.context: Opened 1995, $2B over budget. Confirmed underground baggage system. Murals depicting apocalyptic scenes.

entities.name: Mount Weather Emergency Operations Center
entities.context: FEMA continuity of government facility. Underground city with own power, water, transit system. Used on 9/11.

entities.name: Raven Rock Mountain Complex (Site R)
entities.context: Alternate Pentagon. Built 1950-53. Underground city inside granite mountain. Cheney operated from here on 9/11.

entities.name: Cheyenne Mountain Complex
entities.context: NORAD/USNORTHCOM. Built 1961-66. 15 spring-mounted buildings inside mountain. 25-ton blast doors. Reactivated 2015.

entities.name: Pine Gap (Joint Defence Facility)
entities.context: Joint US/Australian intelligence facility. Largest ECHELON station in Southern Hemisphere. Satellite ground control.

entities.name: White Sands Missile Range
entities.context: Largest US military installation (3,200 sq mi). First nuclear detonation (Trinity). Underground testing facilities. Adjacent Holloman AFB.
entities.notes: Round 1 used the Trinity fireball plus a USGS map. Pass 2 candidates show the actual range terrain and the STS-3 Space Shuttle landing at Northrup Strip (only Shuttle landing at White Sands, 1982).

entities.name: Edwards Air Force Base
entities.context: Air Force Flight Test Center. Extensive underground facilities reported by former contractors. Connected to classified aerospace programs.
entities.notes: Round 1 was an F-35 cockpit-level shot. Pass 2 shows the actual base — overhead Main Base Area aerial, Rogers Dry Lake runway, and the dry lake bed itself (the test-flight signature feature).

entities.name: Vandenberg Space Force Base
entities.context: Space launch facility. Underground missile silos (decommissioned ICBMs). Active satellite launch operations.

entities.name: Camp David (Naval Support Facility Thurmont)
entities.context: Presidential retreat since FDR. Underground bunker facilities. Part of COG network with Mt. Weather and Raven Rock.
entities.notes: Camp David is heavily restricted airspace (P-40 prohibited zone) — no aerial/exterior photos exist on Commons. Best available are exterior shots of the lodge buildings, distinct from round-1 Laurel Cabin interior.

entities.name: Manzano Mountain Nuclear Weapons Storage
entities.context: Nuclear weapons storage since 1950. Tunnels bored into Manzano Mountain. Adjacent to Kirtland AFB/Sandia. Underground infrastructure remains.
entities.notes: Category:Manzano_Base on Commons is empty — facility is not directly photographed. Candidates are aerials of Kirtland AFB (Manzano sits inside the Kirtland reservation) and the adjacent Sandia/Manzano mountain ridge.

entities.name: Wright-Patterson AFB
entities.context: National Air and Space Intelligence Center. Foreign Technology Division. Hangar 18 lore. Goldwater confirmed denied access.

entities.name: The Greenbrier Bunker
entities.context: Congressional relocation facility built 1958. 112,000 sq ft beneath Greenbrier resort. Decommissioned after 1992 expose.

entities.name: Nellis AFB / Nevada Test & Training Range
entities.context: Encompasses Nevada National Security Site (formerly NTS). 928 nuclear tests created extensive underground infrastructure. Yucca Mountain adjacent.
entities.notes: From Category:Nevada_Test_and_Training_Range — Mud Lake landing puts the desert range itself in frame, more evocative than round-1's hangar shot.

entities.name: Dugway Proving Ground
entities.context: Chemical/biological defense testing. 800,000 acres in Utah desert. Multiple restricted underground facilities.

entities.name: Peterson SFB (NORAD Alternate)
entities.context: NORAD and USNORTHCOM headquarters since 2006. Alternate command center after Cheyenne Mountain went to warm standby.

entities.name: Archuleta Mesa
entities.context: Adjacent to Dulce. Bennewitz claimed signal intercepts; AFOSI involvement (Doty) — disinformation op complicates assessment.
entities.notes: No real-world photo of the alleged base; Archuleta County category empty. Candidates show the surrounding Jicarilla Apache Nation landscape and Dulce Lake — area context, no fabrication.

entities.name: The Pentagon
entities.context: DoD (US) — US Department of Defense HQ. Largest office building in the world.

entities.name: Fort Liberty
entities.context: US Army (US) — Home of XVIII Airborne Corps, 82nd Airborne Division. Formerly Fort Bragg.

entities.name: Naval Station Norfolk
entities.context: USN (US) — World's largest naval station. Home to Atlantic Fleet.

entities.name: Joint Base Pearl Harbor-Hickam
entities.context: USN / USAF (US) — USINDOPACOM HQ. Combined naval and air operations.

entities.name: Cheyenne Mountain SFS
entities.context: USSF / NORAD (US) — NORAD and USNORTHCOM alternate command center. Hardened bunker.

entities.name: Wright-Patterson AFB
entities.context: USAF (US) — Air Force Materiel Command HQ. NASIC. Foreign technology evaluation.

entities.name: Edwards Air Force Base
entities.context: USAF (US) — Air Force Test Center. Flight test capital of the world.

entities.name: Eglin Air Force Base
entities.context: USAF (US) — Air Force Materiel Command. Largest AF installation by area.

entities.name: Vandenberg Space Force Base
entities.context: USSF (US) — Space Launch Delta 30. West Coast space launch.

entities.name: Cape Canaveral Space Force Station
entities.context: USSF (US) — East Coast space launch. SLC-40, SLC-41.

entities.name: Minot Air Force Base
entities.context: USAF (US) — B-52H bombers and Minuteman III ICBMs. Dual nuclear mission.

entities.name: Whiteman Air Force Base
entities.context: USAF (US) — Home of B-2 Spirit stealth bomber fleet.

entities.name: NAS Patuxent River
entities.context: USN (US) — Naval Air Systems Command. Aircraft testing and development.

entities.name: MCB Quantico
entities.context: USMC (US) — Crossroads of the Marine Corps. OCS, TBS, FBI Academy co-located.

entities.name: Camp Pendleton
entities.context: USMC (US) — I Marine Expeditionary Force. Largest West Coast expeditionary base.

entities.name: Ramstein Air Base
entities.context: USAF / NATO (DE) — USAFE-AFAFRICA HQ. NATO Allied Air Command.

entities.name: Kadena Air Base
entities.context: USAF (JP) — Largest USAF base in the Pacific. 18th Wing.

entities.name: Yokota Air Base
entities.context: USAF (JP) — USFJ and 5th Air Force HQ. Western Tokyo.

entities.name: Misawa Air Base
entities.context: USAF (JP) — 35th Fighter Wing. F-16s. NSA SIGINT facility co-located.

entities.name: Fleet Activities Yokosuka
entities.context: USN (JP) — US 7th Fleet HQ. Only forward-deployed carrier (CVN).

entities.name: Camp Humphreys
entities.context: US Army (KR) — USFK HQ. Largest US overseas military base.

entities.name: Andersen Air Force Base
entities.context: USAF (GU) — Bomber forward presence in Pacific. B-52 and B-2 rotations.

entities.name: Diego Garcia
entities.context: USN / USAF (IO) — Strategic Indian Ocean atoll. Bomber and submarine support.

entities.name: Al Udeid Air Base
entities.context: USAF (QA) — USCENTCOM forward HQ. Combined Air Operations Center.

entities.name: Incirlik Air Base
entities.context: USAF / Turkish AF (TR) — 39th Air Base Wing. Nuclear weapons storage (B61).

entities.name: RAF Lakenheath
entities.context: USAF (GB) — 48th Fighter Wing. F-15E and F-35A. Only US F-35 base in Europe.

entities.name: NAS Sigonella
entities.context: USN (IT) — Hub of the Med. P-8 and MQ-4C operations.

entities.name: Naval Station Rota
entities.context: USN (ES) — Aegis Ashore BMD. Forward-deployed destroyers.

entities.name: Camp Lemonnier
entities.context: USN / CJTF-HOA (DJ) — Only permanent US base in Africa. Counter-terrorism hub.

entities.name: Pituffik Space Base
entities.context: USSF (GL) — Northernmost US base. BMEWS missile warning radar. Formerly Thule AB.

entities.name: The Pentagon
entities.context: US Department of Defense headquarters. Houses OUSD(I&S), DIA, and UAP disclosure oversight functions.
entities.notes: Round 1 used a cropped variant. These are full oblique aerials from the Aerial_photographs_of_the_Pentagon Commons category. The 2023 DoD shot (7056x4704 source) is the cleanest classic five-sided view.

entities.name: CIA Headquarters
entities.context: Central Intelligence Agency, Langley VA. ORD (Office of Research & Development) linked to consciousness research programs.
entities.notes: Round 1 used a cropped aerial (LOC 14760v). NARA 546702 is the iconic ground-level facade (3000x2020). Cia-lobby-seal.jpg is the floor-seal shot people most associate with CIA HQ (small at 533x428 but instantly recognizable). George Bush Center for Intelligence is the official entrance signage.

entities.name: NSA Fort Meade
entities.context: National Security Agency headquarters. SIGINT collection, connected to Five Eyes/ECHELON network.

entities.name: DIA / Bolling AFB
entities.context: Defense Intelligence Agency. Ran AAWSAP/AATIP ($22M, 2007-2012). 38 Defense Intelligence Reference Documents (DIRDs).

entities.name: Battelle Memorial Institute
entities.context: Manages 7 national labs. Named in Grusch testimony and Wilson-Davis memo as materials custodian.

entities.name: Lockheed Skunk Works
entities.context: Lockheed Martin Advanced Development Programs (Palmdale). Named in multiple retrieval program allegations.

entities.name: Wright-Patterson FTD
entities.context: Foreign Technology Division (now NASIC). Historical center for recovered material analysis per Goldwater, Corso.
entities.notes: Round 1 returned a C-17 ramp shot and the NASIC seal — neither shows the building. Pulled from Category:National_Air_and_Space_Intelligence_Center. The first candidate is the actual NASIC building shot (1000x961). The second is a 2017 USAF interior/event shot at NASIC.

entities.name: Lawrence Livermore NL
entities.context: LLNL. Nuclear weapons lab. Connected to Z Division (intelligence). AAWSAP subcontractor.

entities.name: Los Alamos NL
entities.context: LANL. Manhattan Project origin. Active nuclear weapons + exotic materials research.

entities.name: Bigelow Aerospace
entities.context: Robert Bigelow's aerospace company, Las Vegas. Received AAWSAP contract. BAASS subcontractor for UAP investigation.

entities.name: Skinwalker Ranch
entities.context: Uintah Basin, UT. NIDS then BAASS/AAWSAP research site. Anomalous phenomena documented by Kelleher/Knapp.

entities.name: EG&G / Nevada Test Site
entities.context: EG&G Special Projects. Wilson-Davis memo implicates test site contractor in reverse engineering program.

entities.name: US Capitol
entities.context: Congressional oversight. AARO, Grusch testimony (July 2023), UAPDA legislation, Schumer-Rounds amendment.

entities.name: Raytheon Missiles & Defense
entities.context: Raytheon (now RTX) missile systems, Tucson AZ. Named in UAP contractor allegations.

entities.name: Northrop Grumman
entities.context: Northrop Grumman Palmdale facility. B-2/B-21 programs. Named in legacy program allegations.

entities.name: Boeing Phantom Works
entities.context: Boeing classified programs division. St. Louis + satellite locations. Named by Grusch in contractor allegations.

entities.name: SAIC/Leidos
entities.context: Science Applications International. Former employers of multiple UAP researchers. Government IT contractor.

entities.name: Stanford / SRI International
entities.context: SRI International (formerly Stanford Research Institute). Ran Stargate remote viewing program for CIA/DIA.

entities.name: AARO (Pentagon)
entities.context: All-domain Anomaly Resolution Office. Established 2022. Kirkpatrick then Phillips. Congressional reporting mandate.
